title: Here’s your chance to design a new Boston Public Library card
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston Public Library is hosting a card design contest from May 15 to June 15, ending for Library Card Sign-Up Month.
One winner will be chosen from youth, teen, and adult age groups; designs will be featured on library cards.
Submissions must be original, high-resolution, and submitted online by June 15, excluding AI-generated content.

title: ‘This was my baby’: Blaze destroys popular Belmont ice cream shop, causes floor to collapse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A three-alarm fire destroyed Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont on Thursday, causing the floor to collapse.
A ruptured gas line during the fire led to a secondary blaze; one firefighter sustained a minor injury.
The fire's extensive damage makes rebuilding impossible; neighboring businesses suffered smoke and water damage.

title: Patriots officially unveil 2026 schedule that begins with Super Bowl rematch
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots' 2026 season opens with a Super Bowl LX rematch against the Seattle Seahawks on September 9 in Seattle.
New England's schedule includes six nationally televised games, featuring matchups on Thursday, Sunday, and Monday Night Football.
The Patriots will play an international game in Munich, Germany, facing the Detroit Lions on November 15.

title: Did my vehicle miss out on an important safety recall?
author: John Paul
bullets: VW Jetta owner questions missing recall notice for ABS module issue in 2009-2011 models.
Car Doctor advises contacting Volkswagen and NHTSA for possible reimbursement if repair predated recall.
Recalls may not cover all vehicles in a model year; they depend on specific production runs or factors.

title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Assistant GM Jamie Langenbrunner leaves Boston Bruins after 11 years to pursue NHL opportunities.
Langenbrunner joined Bruins as development coach in 2015, promoted to assistant GM in 2022.
Possible successors for Langenbrunner's role include Adam McQuaid and Zdeno Chara.

title: What Sam Kennedy said of Jason Varitek’s future with the Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jason Varitek's future role with the Red Sox is still undecided after recent staff changes.
Red Sox CEO Sam Kennedy expressed hope for Varitek's long-term presence in the organization.
Reports suggest Varitek may not stay with the Red Sox, despite Kennedy's supportive comments.

title: Former Celtics star Isaiah Thomas hired by Boston as a pro, college scout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isaiah Thomas, 37, hired by Boston Celtics as a pro and college scout based in Seattle.
Thomas joined Celtics executives at the NBA Combine in Chicago to interview draft prospects.
Thomas, a former Celtics All-Star, played for various NBA teams after leaving Boston in 2017.

title: This play from Drake Maye’s rookie season continues to stand out to new Patriots safety
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye's standout play as a rookie involved a game-tying touchdown pass against the Titans on Nov. 3, 2024.
New Patriots safety Mike Brown praised Maye's performance, recalling the play while he was with the Tennessee Titans.
New tight end Julian Hill expressed excitement about collaborating with fellow North Carolina native and quarterback Drake Maye.

title: Worcester man goes viral for performance protesting potholes at city council meeting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Worcester resident Wilson Lam went viral after a theatrical protest for pothole repairs at a city council meeting.
Lam's TikTok performance, mimicking vehicle-pothole sounds, received over 51,800 likes and 402,200 views.
Lam plans to run for Worcester mayor in 2027, with a key focus on fixing city potholes.

title: Jared Wilson’s switch back to center is a ‘full-circle moment’ with Drake Maye
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jared Wilson is returning to center role for the New England Patriots, reuniting with quarterback Drake Maye.
Wilson played left guard as a rookie but was originally a center drafted from the University of Georgia in 2025.
The Patriots traded veteran center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears and added Alijah Vera-Tucker to the offensive line.
